shower.thought: We spend our whole lives trying to be remembered, but we'll never actually experience being remembered.
The things you're most ashamed of are usually the things that make you most human to other people.
You can't miss something you've never had, but you can definitely grieve it.
Everyone thinks they're the reasonable one in the argument.
Your childhood felt like it lasted forever, but you can barely account for where the last decade went.
The people who hurt us most are usually the ones we let closest, which means we might be choosing our own pain.
You're always the most honest version of yourself when you're talking to someone you don't plan to see again.
Every time you learn something new, you have to accept that you were wrong before—and most people do that less than they'd admit.
The scariest part of falling asleep is that last moment where you lose control of your thoughts, but you do it willingly every night.
You'll never know if someone actually likes you or just likes the idea of you.
Boredom might be the feeling of your brain begging you to change something, and you keep ignoring it.
The only time you're fully present is when you're in genuine danger or genuine joy—everything else is just you waiting for one of those things.
We call it self-care, but it's mostly just maintenance to keep ourselves functional enough to tolerate our lives.
You've probably given advice that changed someone's life without ever knowing it happened.
Your biggest insecurity is probably something nobody else has ever noticed about you.
The moment you stop caring what people think is often the moment they start respecting you more.
We treat our bodies like rental cars we don't own—we'll trash them and deal with it later.
Every person you've ever met is the main character of a story you're not allowed to read.
You can't actually imagine not existing, so the concept of death is something your brain just gives up on.
Nostalgia is just your brain editing out all the bad parts and keeping the good—which means your memory is basically fiction.

motivational.thought: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now. — Chinese Proverb
Your life is not a dress rehearsal. Stop waiting for the main event. — Unknown
Comparison is the thief of progress. Focus on your own lane. — Unknown
What we resist persists. What we accept and act on transforms. — Carl Jung
You miss 100% of the shots you don't take. — Wayne Gretzky
Discomfort is the price of admission to a better life. — Unknown
The difference between who you are and who you want to be is what you do. — Unknown
Courage is not the absence of fear. It's doing it anyway. — Unknown
Your future self is watching you right now. Make them proud. — Unknown
Done is better than perfect. Perfect is the enemy of done. — Voltaire
The only person you're destined to become is the person you decide to be. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Pain is temporary. Regret lasts forever. — Unknown
You don't rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems. — Unknown
Every expert was once a beginner who refused to quit. — Unknown
The obstacle is the way. — Marcus Aurelius
If it scares you, it might be a good thing to try. — Seth Godin
Momentum builds character. Start moving, even if it's small. — Unknown
Your circumstances don't define you. Your response to them does. — Unknown
Growth lives on the other side of comfort. — Unknown
The cost of discipline is always less than the cost of regret. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
I told my girlfriend she was drawing her eyebrows too high.
She looked surprised.

My therapist asked if I ever thought about ending it all.
I said "Every Sunday around 6 PM when I remember I have work tomorrow."

I'm reading a book on the history of glue.
Can't put it down.

My dad said he was going to leave me his entire estate.
Turns out he just meant the house is haunted now.

I asked my accountant if I could deduct my emotional damage as a business expense.
He said "Only if you're a therapist."

My friend said his new job was soul-crushing.
I said "At least you have a job. Mine doesn't even bother crushing my soul anymore—it's just indifferent to me."

I went to a self-help seminar called "How to Let Go of the Past."
Halfway through I realized I couldn't afford the ticket and I've been financially anxious ever since.

My grandmother asked me why I wasn't married yet.
I said "I'm waiting for someone who'll love me despite my personality."
She said "Honey, that's called a hostage."

I told my boss I needed a mental health day.
He said "Sure, take the whole month."
Turns out he wasn't being generous, just practical.

My dating profile says I'm fluent in sarcasm.
So far it's only matched me with people who think I'm serious and people who are worse at it than I am.

I asked the pharmacist if my antidepressants were working.
He said "Well, you're here complaining about it instead of not being here, so I'd call that a win."

My mother-in-law told me I'm not good enough for her daughter.
I said "I know, but apparently I'm good enough for the next 40 years."

I went to a funeral for someone who didn't believe in funerals.
It was the least attended party of his life, which tracks.

My therapist said I need to work on my self-esteem.
I said "I would, but I don't think I'm capable of it."
She wrote that down like I'd just said something profound.

I told my friend I was ghosting my gym membership.
He said "That's what I do with people."
I said "Yeah, I know."

My doctor said I needed to cut back on caffeine.
I asked if that was medical advice or just him being tired of me asking him questions.

I applied for a job and they said I was overqualified.
Finally, a place that recognizes my one real talent: disappointing everyone, including employers.

My wife asked me what I wanted for my birthday.
I said "Anything, really, I'm not picky."
She got me a calendar for next year, which was honest.

I told my kid that Santa isn't real.
He said "I know, I've been faking sleep to keep you from finding my browser history."

My priest said God has a plan for me.
I said "Does it involve me understanding it at any point?"
He said "Definitely not."
